title: Authors and Citation
text: Chris Soria. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Soria, C. (2026). Scaling Open-Ended Survey Coding: LLM Pipeline Definitions Heavy Lifting. SocArXiv. https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/gjvcf_v1 Soria, C. (2026). CatLLM: Reproducible Python Ecosystem Generating, Assigning, Scoring Open-Ended Text, Images, Documents Across Research Domains (v3.0.0) [Software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19960067
code: @Article{,   title = {Scaling Open-Ended Survey Coding: An {LLM} Pipeline Where Definitions Do the Heavy Lifting},   author = {Chris Soria},   journal = {SocArXiv},   year = {2026},   url = {https://osf.io/preprints/socarxiv/gjvcf_v1}, } @Misc{,   title = {{cat.stack}: General-Purpose LLM Text Classification Engine},   author = {Chris Soria},   year = {2026},   publisher = {Zenodo},   doi = {10.5281/zenodo.19960067},   url = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19960067},   note = {R package version 0.2.1. Part of the CatLLM ecosystem.}, }

what: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
text: General-purpose LLM text classification engine R. base package CatLLM R ecosystem, providing domain-agnostic classification, extraction, exploration, summarization text, images, PDFs using large language models. cat.stack wraps Python cat-stack package via reticulate. makes domain assumptions can used classification task.

title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
code: # From R-universe (recommended) install.packages("cat.stack",                  repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",                           "https://cloud.r-project.org"))  # Or from a local clone devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack")  # Install the Python backend (one-time setup) cat.stack::install_cat_stack()  # With PDF support cat.stack::install_cat_stack(pdf = TRUE)

title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
code: library(cat.stack)  results <- classify(   input_data  = c("I love this product!", "Terrible experience.", "It was fine."),   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   description = "Customer feedback sentiment",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
code: result <- extract(   input_data  = df$responses,   description = "Why did you move to this city?",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) print(result$top_categories)

title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
code: results <- summarize(   input_data   = df$articles,   description  = "News articles",   instructions = "Provide a 2-sentence summary of each article",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
code: results <- classify(   input_data  = df$responses,   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   models      = list(     c("gpt-4o",              "openai",    Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")),     c("claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929", "anthropic", Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))   ),   consensus_threshold = "unanimous" )

title: cat.stack — General-purpose LLM text classification for R
text: MIT

what: Check whether a specific Ollama model is installed locally — check_ollama_model
title: Check whether a specific Ollama model is installed locally — check_ollama_model
text: Returns TRUE named model available local Ollama installation, FALSE otherwise. Partial name matching supported (e.g. "llama3.2" matches "llama3.2:latest").

code: check_ollama_model(model, host = "localhost", port = 11434L)

text: model Character. Model name look (e.g. "qwen2.5:7b"). host Character. Hostname Ollama reachable . Default "localhost". port Integer. Port Ollama reachable . Default 11434L.

title: Check whether a specific Ollama model is installed locally — check_ollama_model
text: Logical scalar.

title: Check whether a specific Ollama model is installed locally — check_ollama_model

what: Classify text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — classify
title: Classify text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — classify
text: Wraps Python cat_stack.classify() function. Supports single-model multi-model (ensemble) classification.

title: Classify text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — classify
code: classify(   input_data,   categories,   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   user_model = "gpt-4o",   mode = "image",   creativity = NULL,   safety = FALSE,   chain_of_verification = FALSE,   chain_of_thought = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   thinking_budget = 0L,   example1 = NULL,   example2 = NULL,   example3 = NULL,   example4 = NULL,   example5 = NULL,   example6 = NULL,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 10L,   research_question = NULL,   models = NULL,   consensus_threshold = "unanimous",   survey_question = "",   use_json_schema = TRUE,   max_workers = NULL,   fail_strategy = "partial",   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 1L,   json_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   pdf_dpi = 150L,   auto_download = FALSE,   add_other = "prompt",   check_verbosity = TRUE,   multi_label = TRUE,   batch_mode = FALSE,   batch_poll_interval = 30,   batch_timeout = 86400,   json_formatter = NULL,   two_step_classify = NULL,   embedding_tiebreaker = FALSE,   min_centroid_size = 3L,   auto_start_ollama = TRUE,   system_prompt = "",   prompt_tune = NULL,   tune_iterations = 1L,   tune_ui = "browser",   tune_optimize = "balanced" )

title: Classify text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — classify
text: input_data character vector, list text strings, data.frame column containing items classify. image PDF classification, directory path character vector file paths. categories character vector category names, "auto" infer categories data (requires survey_question). api_key API key model provider (single-model mode). required models supplied. description Character. Context description classification task (e.g., survey question image subject). Default "". user_model Character. Model name use single-model mode. Default "gpt-4o". mode Character. PDF processing mode: "image" (default), "text", "". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature setting (0-2). NULL uses provider default. Default NULL. safety Logical. TRUE, saves progress item. Default FALSE. chain_of_verification Logical. Enable Chain Verification. Empirically degrades accuracy – provided research . Default FALSE. chain_of_thought Logical. Enable chain--thought reasoning. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Enable step-back prompting. Default FALSE. context_prompt Logical. Add expert context prompts. Default FALSE. thinking_budget Integer. Extended thinking token budget (0 = ). Default 0L. example1, example2, example3, example4, example5, example6 Optional -shot example strings. Empirically degrades accuracy – provided research . filename Character NULL. Output CSV filename. Default NULL. save_directory Character NULL. Directory save results. Default NULL. model_source Character. Provider hint single-model mode: "auto", "openai", "anthropic", "google", "mistral", "perplexity", "huggingface", "xai", "ollama", "claude-code". Default "auto" (detects model name; falls back "huggingface" Qwen/Llama/DeepSeek-style names — use "ollama" explicitly route local Ollama server). max_categories Integer. Maximum number categories categories = "auto". Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Categories extracted per chunk categories = "auto". Default 10L. divisions Integer. Number data chunks categories = "auto". Default 10L. research_question Character NULL. Optional research context. Default NULL. models list model specifications multi-model ensemble mode. element either 3-element character vector c("model", "provider", "api_key") 4-element list list("model", "provider", "api_key", list(creativity = 0.5)). models supplied, api_key user_model ignored. consensus_threshold Character numeric. Agreement threshold ensemble mode. Options: "unanimous" (default, 100% — empirically accurate) "majority" — STRICT majority. half models must vote positive. Ties (50/50 splits even-model ensembles like 2-2 4) resolve "0". matches sklearn's VotingClassifier default standard ensemble literature. 2-model ensembles, "majority" effectively requires models agree positive ("half" 2 without ); use 3+ models non-degenerate majority vote, pass 0.5 numerically keep old "tie favors positive" semantics. "two-thirds" — ~67% agreement, >= semantics. numeric 0 1 — evaluated >= semantics (user picked number; get literal interpretation). output data.frame multi-model runs includes category_N_agreement columns (fraction models match consensus, 0.0-1.0). even-model ensembles "majority", pair embedding_tiebreaker = TRUE resolve true 50/50 ties via embedding-centroid similarity instead default "tie → 0"; adds category_N_resolved_by audit column (values: "vote" "centroid"). survey_question Character. survey question text (used categories = "auto"). Default "". use_json_schema Logical. Use JSON schema structured output. Default TRUE. max_workers Integer NULL. Max parallel workers. NULL = auto. Default NULL. fail_strategy Character. handle failures: "partial" (default) "strict". max_retries Integer. Max retries per API call. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Max retries batch-level failures. Default 1L. Note: composes multiplicatively json_retries — row can hit LLM (1 + json_retries) * (1 + batch_retries) times. json_retries Integer. Per-row retries LLM returns JSON fails schema validation. retry prompt appends "Respond valid JSON". final attempt formatter fallback (enabled via json_formatter) fires row marked failed. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Seconds retries. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Seconds processing row (useful rate limiting). Default 0.0. pdf_dpi Integer. DPI PDF page rendering. Default 150L. auto_download Logical. Auto-download Ollama models. Default FALSE. add_other Logical "prompt". Controls auto-addition "" catch-category. "prompt" (default) asks interactively – non-interactive sessions silently defaults "". TRUE silently adds "". FALSE never adds . check_verbosity Logical. Check whether category description examples (1 API call). Default TRUE. multi_label Logical. TRUE (default), prompt allows multiple categories per response. FALSE, prompt instructs model assign exactly one best-matching category (single-label). batch_mode Logical. TRUE, use async batch APIs ~50% cost savings higher rate limits. Supported providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, xAI. HuggingFace / Perplexity / Ollama fall back synchronous calls. Incompatible PDF / image input embedding_tiebreaker. Default FALSE. batch_poll_interval Numeric. Seconds batch-job status polls batch_mode = TRUE. Default 30.0. batch_timeout Numeric. Maximum seconds wait batch job complete. Default 86400.0 (24 hours). json_formatter TRUE, FALSE, NULL. Three-state control local JSON-repair fallback model fixes malformed LLM output marking rows failed. Runs extract_json() produces invalid output. model (~1 GB) downloaded HuggingFace Hub first use; requires cat-stack[formatter]. TRUE — eagerly load use formatter (implicit consent ~1.5 GB dependency install needed). FALSE — disabled; malformed rows stay failures. NULL (default) — auto-prompt first malformed row. dependencies installed, asks "Use formatter run? (Y/n)"; , asks "Download deps (~1.5 GB) use formatter? (Y/n)". Non-TTY contexts (CI, batch scripts) decline silently print one-time suggestion. Auto-enabled two_step_classify = TRUE model uses Ollama provider. two_step_classify TRUE, FALSE, NULL. Split classification two LLM calls — (1) natural-language reasoning, (2) JSON formatting. reliable weaker models (local Ollama, lower-tier API models like gpt-4o-mini, claude-haiku-4-5, gemini-2.5-flash) struggle produce strict per-category JSON single shot. Default NULL (auto-enables Ollama models, disabled otherwise). enabled, json_formatter auto-enabled . embedding_tiebreaker Logical. Resolve true ensemble ties (50/50 splits threshold) using embedding centroids built unanimously-agreed rows; closer centroid wins. Companion consensus_threshold = "majority" even-model ensembles — replaces default "tie → 0" evidence-based decision. Adds category_N_resolved_by audit column output (values: "vote" "centroid"). Multi-model ensemble + text input ; supported batch_mode. Requires cat-stack[embeddings]. Default FALSE. min_centroid_size Integer. Minimum number unanimously-agreed rows needed build centroid category embedding_tiebreaker = TRUE. Categories fewer confident rows fall back vote-based consensus. Default 3L. auto_start_ollama Logical. TRUE (default), automatically call ensure_ollama_running() model_source = "ollama" ensemble entry uses "ollama" provider. Set FALSE skip check (e.g. CI runners want launch Ollama). system_prompt Character. Custom system-level instruction prepended every classification call. Use apply prompt returned prompt_tune(): system_prompt = result$system_prompt. Default "". prompt_tune Integer NULL. set, enables Automatic Prompt Optimization (APO). value number rows sampled per correction round. browser window opens can correct misclassifications; meta-LLM rewrites system prompt re-classifies accuracy converges tune_iterations reached. Categories never modified — system prompt changes. Default NULL (disabled). tune_iterations Integer. Number APO optimization passes. Default 1L. tune_ui Character. Correction UI: "browser" (default) opens interactive browser window; "terminal" uses console. tune_optimize Character. Metric optimize: "balanced" (default, maximizes average accuracy, sensitivity, precision), "sensitivity" (minimize false negatives), "precision" (minimize false positives).

title: Classify text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — classify
text: data.frame one row per input item classification columns. single-model mode columns category names. ensemble mode additional consensus_* agreement_* columns included.

title: Classify text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — classify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single-model classification results <- classify(   input_data  = c("I love this!", "Terrible service.", "It was okay."),   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   description = "Customer feedback",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Single-label: force exactly one best-matching category per response # (the prompt asks for the single most appropriate category instead of # all that apply). Use for mutually exclusive coding frames. results <- classify(   input_data  = c("I love this!", "Terrible service.", "It was okay."),   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   description = "Customer feedback",   multi_label = FALSE,   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # Multi-model ensemble results <- classify(   input_data  = df$responses,   categories  = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   models      = list(     c("gpt-4o",              "openai",    Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")),     c("claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929", "anthropic", Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))   ),   consensus_threshold = "unanimous" )  # Even-model ensemble with strict-majority + embedding tiebreaker # (resolves true 50/50 ties via centroid similarity instead of # the default "tie -> 0"; requires cat-stack[embeddings]) results <- classify(   input_data           = df$responses,   categories           = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   models               = list(     c("gpt-4o-mini",      "openai",    Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")),     c("claude-haiku-4-5", "anthropic", Sys.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))   ),   consensus_threshold  = "majority",   embedding_tiebreaker = TRUE )  # Async batch mode (50% cheaper, slower) — OpenAI / Anthropic / # Google / Mistral / xAI only; not yet supported with PDFs/images # or embedding_tiebreaker. results <- classify(   input_data = df$responses,   categories = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   batch_mode = TRUE ) } # }

title: Convert R models list to Python tuples — .convert_models

code: .convert_models(models)

what: Strip surrounding quotes from a string — .strip_quotes
title: Strip surrounding quotes from a string — .strip_quotes
text: Many .env files wrap values single double quotes (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."). Python's dotenv strips automatically, R users read .env files manually may inadvertently pass quotes . helper removes .

title: Strip surrounding quotes from a string — .strip_quotes
code: .strip_quotes(x)

title: Strip surrounding quotes from a string — .strip_quotes
text: x character scalar.

title: Strip surrounding quotes from a string — .strip_quotes
text: x leading/trailing matching quotes removed.

what: Validate and normalise the add_other argument — .validate_add_other
title: Validate and normalise the add_other argument — .validate_add_other
text: Python's default add_other "prompt", calls input(). non-interactive sessions, Python's input() raises EOFError, Python code catches treats "". R default matches Python's default ("prompt"). helper validates user's input.

title: Validate and normalise the add_other argument — .validate_add_other
code: .validate_add_other(add_other)

title: Validate and normalise the add_other argument — .validate_add_other
text: add_other FALSE, TRUE, "prompt".

title: Validate and normalise the add_other argument — .validate_add_other
text: validated value (unchanged already valid).

what: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
title: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
text: Checks whether Ollama server reachable host:port. , attempts start using platform-appropriate command polls server responds (timeout reached). Call top R session classifying model_source = "ollama".

title: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
code: ensure_ollama_running(   auto_start = TRUE,   timeout = 30,   host = "localhost",   port = 11434L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
text: auto_start Logical. TRUE (default), attempt launch Ollama running. FALSE, just check error running. timeout Numeric. Seconds wait Ollama become ready auto_start. Default 30. host Character. Hostname Ollama reachable . Default "localhost". port Integer. Port Ollama reachable . Default 11434L. verbose Logical. Print status messages. Default TRUE.

title: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
text: Invisibly returns TRUE Ollama running.

title: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
text: Platform start commands: macOS — open -Ollama (launches Ollama.app daemon). Falls back ollama serve app installed. Linux — ollama serve (run detached process). Windows — ollama serve. Ollama installed, function returns clear error message linking https://ollama.com.

title: Ensure a local Ollama server is running — ensure_ollama_running
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Ensure Ollama is up before classifying with a local model ensure_ollama_running()  results <- classify(   input_data   = c("text 1", "text 2"),   categories   = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   user_model   = "qwen2.5:7b",   model_source = "ollama" )  # Just check without auto-starting ensure_ollama_running(auto_start = FALSE) } # }

what: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
title: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
text: Wraps Python cat_stack.explore() function. Returns every category string extracted every chunk across every iteration – duplicates intact. Useful analysing category stability saturation across repeated extraction runs.

title: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
code: explore(   input_data,   api_key,   description = "",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 12L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   specificity = "broad",   research_question = NULL,   filename = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   iterations = 8L,   random_state = NULL,   focus = NULL,   chunk_delay = 0,   auto_start_ollama = TRUE )

title: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
text: input_data character vector, list, data.frame column text responses. api_key Character. API key model provider. description Character. survey question data description. Default "". max_categories Integer. Maximum categories per chunk. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Categories extract per chunk. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Number data chunks. Default 12L. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature setting. NULL uses provider default. Default NULL. specificity Character. "broad" (default) "specific". research_question Character NULL. Optional research context. filename Character NULL. Optional CSV filename save raw category list. model_source Character. Provider hint. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Number passes data. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. focus Character NULL. Optional focus instruction. chunk_delay Numeric. Seconds API calls. Default 0.0. auto_start_ollama Logical. TRUE (default), automatically call ensure_ollama_running() model_source = "ollama". Set FALSE skip check (e.g. CI).

title: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
text: character vector every category string extracted across chunks iterations. Length approximately iterations * divisions * categories_per_chunk.

title: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
text: Unlike extract(), normalises deduplicates categories, explore() returns raw unprocessed output suitable frequency saturation analysis.

title: Explore raw categories in text data — explore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ raw_cats <- explore(   input_data  = df$responses,   description = "Why did you move?",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   iterations  = 3L,   divisions   = 5L ) length(raw_cats)   # ~150 head(raw_cats, 10) } # }

what: Extract categories from text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — extract
title: Extract categories from text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — extract
text: Wraps Python cat_stack.extract() function. Discovers returns normalised, deduplicated set categories found input data.

title: Extract categories from text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — extract
code: extract(   input_data,   api_key,   input_type = "text",   description = "",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 12L,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   creativity = NULL,   specificity = "broad",   research_question = NULL,   mode = "text",   filename = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   iterations = 8L,   random_state = NULL,   focus = NULL,   chunk_delay = 0,   auto_start_ollama = TRUE )

title: Extract categories from text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — extract
text: input_data character vector, list, data.frame column. images/PDFs, directory path character vector file paths. api_key Character. API key model provider. input_type Character. Type input: "text" (default), "image", "pdf". description Character. survey question data description. Default "". max_categories Integer. Maximum number final categories return. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Categories extract per data chunk. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Number chunks divide data . Default 12L. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature setting. NULL uses provider default. Default NULL. specificity Character. Category granularity: "broad" (default) "specific". research_question Character NULL. Optional research context. mode Character. Processing mode. PDFs: "text" (default), "image", "". images: "image" (default) "". filename Character NULL. Optional CSV filename save results. model_source Character. Provider hint: "auto", "openai", "anthropic", "google", etc. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Number passes data. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. focus Character NULL. Optional focus extraction (e.g., "decisions move"). chunk_delay Numeric. Seconds API calls (rate limiting). Default 0.0. auto_start_ollama Logical. TRUE (default), automatically call ensure_ollama_running() model_source = "ollama". Set FALSE skip check (e.g. CI).

title: Extract categories from text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — extract
text: named list : counts_df data.frame discovered categories counts. top_categories character vector top category names. raw_top_text raw model output final merge step.

title: Extract categories from text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — extract
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- extract(   input_data  = df$responses,   description = "Why did you move to this city?",   api_key     = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) print(result$top_categories) print(result$counts_df) } # }

what: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
title: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
text: Installs cat-stack Python package Python environment used reticulate. Optionally installs PDF extras.

title: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
code: install_cat_stack(   method = "auto",   conda = "auto",   pdf = FALSE,   upgrade = FALSE,   ... )

title: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
text: method Installation method passed reticulate::py_install(). Default "auto". conda Conda environment name. Default "auto". pdf Logical. TRUE, installs cat-stack[pdf] PDF extras. Default FALSE. upgrade Logical. TRUE, upgrades existing installation. Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed reticulate::py_install().

title: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
text: Invisibly NULL.

title: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
text: version floor pinned cat-stack >= 1.6.0 — release adds strict-majority consensus, embedding tiebreaker, async batch mode, JSON-formatter auto-consent flow R wrappers now expose. Older Python installs work, R users hit "unexpected keyword argument" errors reticulate new parameters get forwarded.

title: Install the cat-stack Python package — install_cat_stack
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Standard install install_cat_stack()  # With PDF support (installs cat-stack[pdf]) install_cat_stack(pdf = TRUE)  # Upgrade an existing install install_cat_stack(upgrade = TRUE) } # }

what: List locally installed Ollama models — list_ollama_models
title: List locally installed Ollama models — list_ollama_models
text: Returns names models already downloaded local Ollama installation. Requires Ollama running (call ensure_ollama_running() first, start manually ollama serve).

title: List locally installed Ollama models — list_ollama_models
code: list_ollama_models(host = "localhost", port = 11434L)

title: List locally installed Ollama models — list_ollama_models
text: host Character. Hostname Ollama reachable . Default "localhost". port Integer. Port Ollama reachable . Default 11434L.

title: List locally installed Ollama models — list_ollama_models
text: character vector model names (e.g. c("qwen2.5:7b", "mistral:7b")), empty character vector Ollama running.

title: List locally installed Ollama models — list_ollama_models
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ensure_ollama_running() list_ollama_models() } # }

what: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
title: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
text: Wraps Python catstack.prompt_tune() function. Runs coordinate-descent loop: classifies small sample, asks correct model's output, meta-LLM rewrite classification instructions category errors. Returns best system prompt found plus per-iteration metrics.

title: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
code: prompt_tune(   input_data,   categories,   api_key = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   model_source = "auto",   models = NULL,   description = "",   survey_question = "",   sample_size = 10L,   max_iterations = 3L,   multi_label = TRUE,   creativity = NULL,   use_json_schema = TRUE,   consensus_threshold = "unanimous",   max_retries = 5L,   input_mode = NULL,   ui = "terminal",   optimize = "balanced",   add_other = "prompt",   thinking_budget = 0L,   auto_start_ollama = TRUE )

title: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
text: input_data character vector, list, data.frame column items classify tuning. categories character vector category names. labels never modified tuning — classification instructions change. api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". model_source Character. Provider hint. Default "auto". models List model specs ensemble mode (c(model, provider, api_key)). Overrides user_model/api_key/ model_source given. Default NULL. description Character. Context description. Default "". survey_question Character. Survey question context. Default "". sample_size Integer. Items test per iteration. Default 10L. max_iterations Integer. Max instruction attempts per category. Default 3L. multi_label Logical. Multi-label classification. Default TRUE. creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature. Default NULL. use_json_schema Logical. Default TRUE. consensus_threshold Character numeric. ensemble mode. Default "unanimous". max_retries Integer. Default 5L. input_mode Character NULL. Input mode override. ui Character. Review interface corrections. "terminal" (default R) reads stdin. "browser" opens local web page checkboxes (may auto-launch R sessions). optimize Character. metric maximize. "balanced" (default), "precision", "sensitivity". add_other Logical "prompt". Controls auto-addition "" catch-category. Default "prompt". thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. auto_start_ollama Logical. TRUE (default), automatically call ensure_ollama_running() model_source = "ollama" ensemble entry uses "ollama" provider. Set FALSE skip check.

title: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
text: named list components: system_prompt — optimized system prompt (best found) iterations — list per-iteration records (label, system_prompt, metrics, per_category, total_flips) per_category_summary — per-category metrics best-scoring iteration

title: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
text: function interactive — asked review correct model's labels least . R session, default ui = "terminal" reads corrections stdin (works R, Rscript, IDE consoles). ui = "browser" opens local web page checkboxes; depending R setup may may auto-launch browser, terminal safer default R users. Use returned system_prompt classify() via system_prompt = argument apply tuned instructions.

title: Optimize a classification prompt with human-in-the-loop feedback — prompt_tune
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- prompt_tune(   input_data    = df$open_response,   categories    = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini",   sample_size   = 10L,   max_iterations = 3L,   ui            = "terminal" )  # Inspect the optimized prompt cat(result$system_prompt)  # Use it in classify() via the system_prompt argument results <- classify(   input_data    = df$open_response,   categories    = c("Positive", "Negative", "Neutral"),   api_key       = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model    = "gpt-4o-mini",   system_prompt = result$system_prompt ) } # }

title: Pull (download) an Ollama model — pull_ollama_model
text: Downloads named model local Ollama installation. Prints estimated model size resource check downloading. Set auto_confirm = TRUE skip interactive confirmation prompt — useful scripts RMarkdown documents.

title: Pull (download) an Ollama model — pull_ollama_model
code: pull_ollama_model(   model,   host = "localhost",   port = 11434L,   auto_confirm = FALSE )

title: Pull (download) an Ollama model — pull_ollama_model
text: model Character. Model name download (e.g. "llama3.2", "qwen2.5:7b"). host Character. Hostname Ollama reachable . Default "localhost". port Integer. Port Ollama reachable . Default 11434L. auto_confirm Logical. Skip confirmation prompt. Default FALSE.

title: Pull (download) an Ollama model — pull_ollama_model
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success, FALSE failure.

title: Pull (download) an Ollama model — pull_ollama_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pull_ollama_model("llama3.2", auto_confirm = TRUE) } # }

what: Summarize text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — summarize
title: Summarize text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — summarize
text: Wraps Python cat_stack.summarize() function. Generates summaries input data using one LLM models. Supports single-model multi-model (ensemble) summarization.

title: Summarize text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — summarize
code: summarize(   input_data,   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   instructions = "",   format = "paragraph",   max_length = NULL,   focus = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   model_source = "auto",   mode = "image",   input_mode = NULL,   input_type = "auto",   pdf_dpi = 150L,   creativity = NULL,   thinking_budget = 0L,   chain_of_thought = TRUE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   models = NULL,   max_workers = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   auto_download = FALSE,   safety = FALSE,   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 1L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   fail_strategy = "partial",   batch_mode = FALSE,   batch_poll_interval = 30,   batch_timeout = 86400,   auto_start_ollama = TRUE )

title: Summarize text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — summarize
text: input_data character vector, list, data.frame column. images/PDFs, directory path character vector file paths. api_key Character NULL. API key model provider (single-model mode). required models supplied. Default NULL. description Character. Context description summarization task. Default "". instructions Character. Specific instructions summary. Default "". format Character. Output format: "paragraph" (default) supported formats. max_length Integer NULL. Maximum length summary. NULL uses model default. Default NULL. focus Character NULL. Optional focus summary. Default NULL. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". model_source Character. Provider hint: "auto", "openai", "anthropic", "google", etc. Default "auto". mode Character. Processing mode images/PDFs: "image" (default), "text", "". input_mode Character NULL. Explicit input mode override. Default NULL. input_type Character. Type input: "auto" (default), "text", "image", "pdf". pdf_dpi Integer. DPI PDF page rendering. Default 150L. creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature setting. NULL uses provider default. Default NULL. thinking_budget Integer. Extended thinking token budget (0 = ). Default 0L. chain_of_thought Logical. Enable chain--thought reasoning. Default TRUE. context_prompt Logical. Add expert context prompts. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Enable step-back prompting. Default FALSE. filename Character NULL. Output filename. Default NULL. save_directory Character NULL. Directory save results. Default NULL. models list model specifications multi-model ensemble mode. element either 3-element character vector c("model", "provider", "api_key") 4-element list list("model", "provider", "api_key", list(creativity = 0.5)). Default NULL. max_workers Integer NULL. Max parallel workers. NULL = auto. Default NULL. parallel Logical NULL. Enable parallel processing. Default NULL. auto_download Logical. Auto-download Ollama models. Default FALSE. safety Logical. TRUE, saves progress item. Default FALSE. max_retries Integer. Max retries per API call. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Max retries batch-level failures. Default 1L. retry_delay Numeric. Seconds retries. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Seconds processing row. Default 0.0. fail_strategy Character. handle failures: "partial" (default) "strict". batch_mode Logical. Use batch processing mode. Default FALSE. batch_poll_interval Numeric. Seconds batch status polls. Default 30.0. batch_timeout Numeric. Maximum seconds wait batch completion. Default 86400.0. auto_start_ollama Logical. TRUE (default), automatically call ensure_ollama_running() model_source = "ollama" ensemble entry uses "ollama" provider. Set FALSE skip check.

title: Summarize text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — summarize
text: data.frame summarization results.

title: Summarize text, images, or PDFs using LLMs — summarize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single-model summarization results <- summarize(   input_data   = c("A long article about climate change...",                     "A detailed report on economic trends..."),   description  = "News articles",   instructions = "Provide a 2-sentence summary",   api_key      = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )  # PDF summarization results <- summarize(   input_data = "path/to/documents/",   input_type = "pdf",   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) } # }

title: cat.stack 0.2.1
text: multi_label — logical, default TRUE (unchanged behavior). Python engine always supported single-label classification, R wrapper forward flag, classify() ever request multi-label output. Setting multi_label = FALSE now reaches engine switches prompt “assign single appropriate category” (one 1, rest 0), mutually exclusive coding frames.

title: cat.stack 0.2.0
text: Tracks Python cat-stack 1.6.0 release. version floor install_cat_stack() now cat-stack >= 1.6.0 — older Python installs hit unexpected keyword argument errors R forwards new parameters listed .

title: cat.stack 0.2.0
text: batch_mode, batch_poll_interval, batch_timeout — opt async batch APIs (OpenAI / Anthropic / Google / Mistral / xAI) ~50% cost savings higher rate limits. HuggingFace / Perplexity / Ollama fall back synchronous calls. json_retries — per-row retries LLM returns JSON fails schema validation. final attempt formatter fallback fires (json_formatter enabled). json_formatter — three-state (TRUE / FALSE / NULL) control local JSON-repair model. Default NULL triggers interactive consent prompt first malformed row; non-TTY contexts decline silently. Requires cat-stack[formatter]. two_step_classify — split classification reasoning + JSON formatting steps weaker models (lower-tier API + local Ollama). Auto-enables json_formatter set. embedding_tiebreaker + min_centroid_size — resolve true ensemble 50/50 ties via embedding-centroid similarity instead default “tie → 0”. Adds category_N_resolved_by audit column ("vote" "centroid"). Multi-model ensemble + text input ; yet supported batch_mode. Requires cat-stack[embeddings].

title: cat.stack 0.2.0
text: consensus_threshold = "majority" now strict majority. 50/50 tie even-model ensemble (2-2 4, 3-3 6, 1-1 2) resolves "0", "1". Matches sklearn’s VotingClassifier default standard ensemble literature. Numeric thresholds (e.g. consensus_threshold = 0.5) keep >= semantics — user picked number, get literal interpretation. 2-model ensembles, "majority" now effectively requires models agree positive. Use 3+ models non-degenerate majority vote, pair embedding_tiebreaker = TRUE. batch_retries default lowered 2L 1L match Python default (changed cat-stack 1.4.1).

title: cat.stack 0.2.0
text: consensus_threshold man page now describes strict-majority semantics, 2-model degeneracy, numeric-input escape hatch, embedding_tiebreaker companion. Added new example block showing strict-majority + tiebreaker batch_mode = TRUE.

what: Python-side fixes that flow through automatically
title: cat.stack 0.2.0
text: Python cat-stack 1.6.0 pass reticulate bridge without R-side changes — listed users know behaviors ’re getting install_cat_stack(upgrade = TRUE): Google preflight 400 fixed (additionalProperties stripped reaching responseSchema). Per-model batch-job failure isolation — one model’s batch failure longer kills ensemble run. Anthropic batch terminal-state inspection — -errored batches raise instead silently returning empty results. system_prompt longer silently dropped batch_mode. PDF summary synthesis grounds actual page text instead page label. HuggingFace small-model strip--5xx (Llama-3.2-1B / response_format). Image directory loading case-insensitive; large images warn. prompt_tune returns system_prompt = "" improvement found (instead returning non-improving prompt default).

title: cat.stack 0.1.0
text: Initial release R interface Python catstack package. Exposes classify(), extract(), explore(), summarize() domain-agnostic LLM-powered text, image, PDF classification. install_cat_stack() installs Python dependency via reticulate. Internal helpers (.strip_quotes(), .as_py_int(), .convert_models(), .validate_add_other()) exported reuse sibling domain packages (cat.survey, cat.ademic, cat.pol, cat.web, etc.).
